entities.name: INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A holistic approach to contain pests below economically damaging levels using feasible control measures while ensuring environmental and operational safety.

entities.name: LEGUME CROPS
entities.description: Major crops that are the subject of integrated pest management.

entities.name: PEST
entities.description: Organisms that cause economic damage to crops and are the target of containment in IPM.

entities.name: ECONOMICALLY DAMAGING LEVELS
entities.description: The threshold of pest population that causes significant economic loss.

entities.name: INTEGRATED CONTROL
entities.description: An earlier term used to describe the principles of integrated pest management.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The natural surroundings that IPM aims to protect through safe control measures.

entities.name: PUBLIC HEALTH
entities.description: The health of the general population, which is a priority in the promotion of IPM.

entities.name: HOLISTIC APPROACH
entities.description: A comprehensive management strategy that integrates various tools and perspectives.

entities.name: TRADITIONAL AND MODERN TOOLS
entities.description: The set of instruments and techniques used to implement pest management.

entities.name: ECOLOGICAL ASPECTS
entities.description: The environmental and biological interactions focused on in early IPM models.

entities.name: EVOLUTIONARY ASPECTS
entities.description: The adaptive and genetic changes of pests considered in early IPM models.
